assertions.title: Refresh commands handle both refreshable and static accounts clearly

steps.action: Import a refreshable saved Codex account into isolated state and add a static account
steps.expected: Fixture state contains one active refreshable account and one static account
steps.observed: account list showed `refreshable-local` active with `refresh=refreshable` and `static-local` present with `refresh=static`

steps.action: Run `account refresh-codex` with no `--name` while the refreshable account is active
steps.expected: Refresh succeeds through the configured local refresh endpoint and updates saved account metadata
steps.observed: Command printed `refreshed saved Codex account `refreshable-local` in local state`; the local stub received one POST to `/oauth/token`; state updated access token, refresh token, email, account id, plan, and expiry

steps.expected: Static account refresh is rejected with a clear operator-readable error and no state corruption
steps.observed: Command exited non-zero with `account `static-local` is static and has no refresh token`; follow-up state snapshot matched the post-success state byte-for-byte

steps.expected: Missing account refresh is rejected with a clear operator-readable error and no state corruption
steps.observed: Command exited non-zero with `no saved Codex account named missing-local`; follow-up state snapshot remained unchanged and list output still showed the same active/static accounts

summary: VAL-CLI-003 passed: refresh succeeded for the active refreshable Codex account through `codex.refresh_token_url`, static and missing account refreshes failed clearly, and saved state stayed coherent after all runs.
